title: Force unwrap crash in FilePrivilege init parsing permissions file
description: The FilePrivilege failable initializer reads the permissions file and splits its content using a separator character. It then force-unwraps components.first! at line 251 and accesses components[1] at line 252. If the permissions file is malformed (e.g. corrupted, manually edited, or containing no separators), the array could have fewer than 2 elements, causing an index-out-of-bounds crash. Since the permissions file is stored inside .wired/permissions under user-controlled directory paths, an attacker who can write arbitrary files (via upload to a dropbox) could craft a malicious .wired/permissions file to crash the server when any user lists or accesses that directory.
vulnerable_code:
let components = string.split(separator: Character(File.wiredPermissionsFieldSeparator), omittingEmptySubsequences: false)
self.owner  = String(components.first!)
self.group  = String(components[1])
self.mode   = File.FilePermissions(rawValue: UInt32(String(components[2])) ?? 0)
recommendation: Guard that components has at least 3 elements before accessing indices: guard components.count >= 3 else { return nil }. Replace force unwraps with safe indexing.
